etymology.description: 大(person) + 火(fire) = person burned in a sacrificial ceremony -> man burned alive
etymology.interpretation: The character 堇(jǐn, 'man burned alive') combines the imagery of a person 大(dà, 'person') and fire 火(huǒ, 'fire'), illustrating a sacrificial context where a person is burned, historically associated with rituals to invoke rain.
